perturbation.original_text: The Company will be responsible for ensuring that each link embedded within an Online Promotion takes the User to the appropriate area within the Company Site, and that the Company Site functions with reasonable reliability and in a commercially reasonable manner throughout the Term. In particular, the Company agrees that the Company Site will comply with the performance standards set forth in Exhibit B throughout the Term. Any failure by the Company to comply with this paragraph will be deemed to be a material breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will use its best efforts to ensure that each link embedded within an Online Promotion takes the User to an area within the Company Site, and that the Company Site functions with reliability throughout the Term. The Company agrees to make reasonable efforts to ensure the Company Site will substantially comply with the performance standards set forth in Exhibit B throughout the Term. Any failure by the Company to comply with this paragraph will be addressed through good-faith discussions between CNET and the Company.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 3.1, replacing 'will be responsible for ensuring' with 'will use its best efforts,' and stating that failure to comply will be addressed through discussions, introduces ambiguity. The original text declared any failure to comply as a material breach, while the change softens the obligation. This directly contradicts the definition of 'material breach' as initially presented, leading to uncertainty about enforcement and contract stability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company will use its best efforts to ensure that each link embedded within an Online Promotion takes the User to an area within the Company Site, and that the Company Site functions with reliability throughout the Term. The Company agrees to make reasonable efforts to ensure the Company Site will substantially comply with the performance standards set forth in Exhibit B throughout the Term. Any failure by the Company to comply with this paragraph will be addressed through good-faith discussions between CNET and the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that any failure to comply with paragraph 3.1 constitutes a 'material breach'. The changed text replaces 'will be responsible for ensuring' with 'will use its best efforts' and states that any failure to comply will be addressed through 'good-faith discussions'. This introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation, directly contradicting the original definition of a material breach by removing the certainty of consequences for non-compliance. As such the definition of 'material breach' has shifted which breaks contract stability per the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction. The changed text contradicts the initial commitment, making enforcement unclear and leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this agreement "Competing Computer Products Retailer" means any company other than the Company that is engaged in the retail sale of computer products, with the exception of CNET Direct, which operates BuyDirect.com. During the Term, CNET will not enter into more than two other agreements under which CNET receives consideration from a Competing Computer Products Retailer for displaying permanent links to or other fixed promotions for such Competing Computer Products Retailer on any CNET Site; provided that the foregoing will not restrict the display of(a) standard advertisements for any Competing Computer Products Retailer or its products or (b) any promotions within COMPUTERS.COM or within CNET's Snap! Online service (which are expressly excluded from this provision). The parties acknowledge that the foregoing will not prevent CNET from displaying text links and other references to Competing Computer Products Retailers as reasonably necessary to provide appropriate editorial and search related services on the CNET Sites. The Retail Promotions granted to the Company shall be placed in such a way as to provide no more or less prominence to the Company than is provided to any other Competing Computer Retailer signing an agreement with CNET.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this agreement "Competing Computer Products Retailer" means any company that is engaged in the retail sale of computer products, with the exception of CNET Direct, which operates BuyDirect.com. During the Term, CNET will not enter into more than two other agreements under which CNET receives consideration from a Competing Computer Products Retailer for displaying permanent links to or other fixed promotions for such Competing Computer Products Retailer on any CNET Site; provided that the foregoing will not restrict the display of(a) standard advertisements for any Competing Computer Products Retailer or its products or (b) any promotions within COMPUTERS.COM or within CNET's Snap! Online service (which are expressly excluded from this provision). The parties acknowledge that the foregoing will not prevent CNET from displaying text links and other references to Competing Computer Products Retailers as reasonably necessary to provide appropriate editorial and search related services on the CNET Sites. The Retail Promotions granted to the Company shall be placed in such a way as to provide no more or less prominence to the Company than is provided to any other Competing Computer Retailer signing an agreement with CNET.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'other than the Company' from the definition of 'Competing Computer Products Retailer' in Section 5, CNET itself is now included as a Competing Computer Products Retailer. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the subsequent clauses restrict CNET from entering agreements with more than two competing retailers, now including itself. It is unclear if CNET can enter more agreements with itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this agreement "Competing Computer Products Retailer" means any company that is engaged in the retail sale of computer products, with the exception of CNET Direct, which operates BuyDirect.com. During the Term, CNET will not enter into more than two other agreements under which CNET receives consideration from a Competing Computer Products Retailer for displaying permanent links to or other fixed promotions for such Competing Computer Products Retailer on any CNET Site; provided that the foregoing will not restrict the display of(a) standard advertisements for any Competing Computer Products Retailer or its products or (b) any promotions within COMPUTERS.COM or within CNET's Snap! Online service (which are expressly excluded from this provision). The parties acknowledge that the foregoing will not prevent CNET from displaying text links and other references to Competing Computer Products Retailers as reasonably necessary to provide appropriate editorial and search related services on the CNET Sites. The Retail Promotions granted to the Company shall be placed in such a way as to provide no more or less prominence to the Company than is provided to any other Competing Computer Retailer signing an agreement with CNET.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes 'other than the Company' from the definition of 'Competing Computer Products Retailer.' This includes CNET as a Competing Computer Products Retailer. The original clause states CNET will not enter into more than two other agreements under which CNET receives consideration from a Competing Computer Products Retailer. The change means CNET is now included within this restriction (CNET is a Competing Computer Products Retailer). This creates a direct conflict and ambiguity, as CNET now needs to consider whether or not they can enter into agreements with itself, in effect.
